zh.lifeSummary: 當Warin le Latimer於1300年出生在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾布雷布盧可時，他的父親Sir Thomas le Latimer30歲，母親Lora de Hastings31歲。他於1328年之前與Catherine de la Warre在英國結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1349年8月13日在他的家鄉享年49歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾布雷布盧可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Warin le Latimer于1300年出生在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾布雷布盧可时，他的父亲Sir Thomas le Latimer30岁，母亲Lora de Hastings31岁。他于1328年之前与Catherine de la Warre在英国结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1349年8月13日在他的家乡享年49岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾布雷布盧可。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Latimer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a Latinist, a clerk who wrote documents in Latin, from Anglo-Norman French latinier, latim(m)ier. Latin was more or less the universal language of official documents in the Middle Ages, displaced only gradually by the vernacular – in England, by Anglo-Norman French at first, and eventually by English.

variants.name: Latner
meanings: English: occupational name for a worker in or maker of latten or brass, from Middle English latoun ‘brass’ (Old French laton). This name may sometimes have been confused with Latimer .
Altered form of German   Lattner   or   Lettner  .

variants.name: Lattimore
meanings: English: variant of   Latimer  .

variants.name: Latimore
meanings: English: variant of   Latimer  .

variants.name: Lattimer
meanings: English: variant of   Latimer  .

variants.name: Lattner
meanings: English: variant of   Latimer  .
German: occupational name for a carpenter who prepared or used laths or slats, from Middle High German latte ‘slat, lath’ + -n (plural suffix) + the agent suffix -er.

nameMeanings.name: Warin
meanings: An old name. The same as Waring or Guerin, who came over with the Conqueror; a name probably connected with that of the Waernas or Wernas. the Varini or Verini, a people of the Elbe

en.lifeSummary: When Warin le Latimer was born in 1300, in Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Thomas le Latimer, was 30 and his mother, Lora de Hastings, was 31. He married Catherine de la Warre before 1328, in England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 13 August 1349, in his hometown, at the age of 49, and was buried in Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Warin le Latimer est né en 1300, à Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Thomas le Latimer, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Lora de Hastings, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Catherine de la Warre avant 1328, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 13 août 1349, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterré à Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Warin le Latimer 1300 in Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Thomas le Latimer 30 und seine Mutter Lora de Hastings 31. Er hat vor 1328 in England Catherine de la Warre geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 13. August 1349 im Alter von 49 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Warin le Latimer è nato il nel 1300 nel Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Thomas le Latimer, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Lora de Hastings, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Catherine de la Warre prima del 1328, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 13 agosto 1349, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 49 anni, e fu sepolto a Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Warin le Latimerが1300年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアブレイブルークで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas le Latimerは30歳，母Lora de Hastingsは31歳であった。1328年以前にイギリスでCatherine de la Warreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1349年8月13日に故郷で49歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアブレイブルークにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Warin le Latimer(이)가 1300년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 브라이브룩에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas le Latimer(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Lora de Hastings(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1328년 이전 영국에서 Catherine de la Warre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1349년 8월 13일 그의 고향에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 브라이브룩에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Warin le Latimer nasceu em 1300, em Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Thomas le Latimer, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Lora de Hastings, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Catherine de la Warre antes 1328, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 13 de agosto de 1349, em sua cidade natal, com 49 anos, e foi sepultado em Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Warin le Latimer родился в 1300 г. в местности брэибрук, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas le Latimer было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Lora de Hastings, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine de la Warre перед 1328 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 13 августа 1349 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 49 лет и был похоронен в местности брэибрук, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Warin le Latimer nació en 1300, en Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Thomas le Latimer, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Lora de Hastings, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Catherine de la Warre antes de 1328, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 13 de agosto de 1349, en su pueblo, a la edad de 49 años, y fue sepultado en Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
